item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've been reading a lot of Yog-Sothothery lately, for reasons both squamous and rugose, so this hit my crazybone just right. I especially liked the reference to conjured 'shoggettes', which reminded me of the Shoggies from 'Unspeakable Vault'.

And anyone who's read "Name of the Rose" knows not to eat the incunabula.

item.title: Statement of Katherine Sinclair, regarding an “occult manuscript”...
item.content:encoded: Really good. The best part for me was the Magnus Archives reference. Love that spooky podcast.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love the subversion of the "madness diary" cliche with the reveal that it was just drugs. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is just fun to read. It shows the weirder side of the scipcerse really well, there needs to be more pieces like this IMO

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is real good. The logs are great, and I like it as an exploration of the Foundation trying to work out whether or not something is actually anomalous (I assume this technically counts because it does have actually anomalous rituals inside, crappy as they may be!) +1

item.title: This Author Commentary feels like Lovecraft in Brooklyn.
item.content:encoded:
So, for Pulp Fiction, I turned to an SCP about the works of a Pulp Author that most of us can probably recognize. This is… also based off of an actual experience I had.

So, basically, while in London in September of last year, I was wandering around near Leicester square, when I walked into an occult bookstore— they called it an "esoteric" bookstore, but same difference. I was… not impressed in the slightest. For one: the ground floor was dedicated entirely to homeopathic treatments and tarot decks. Did you know that the fucking Beatles have a licensed Tarot deck? I do now. For two, in the basement of the shop, they were selling copies of the Necronomicon. There was no price tag on them. When I asked how much, they said it was fifty pounds, because it was a 'rare edition'. For a third, they had a variety of 'artifacts' that were clearly just cheap brass costume jewelry and shit you could find in the British Museum for a fraction of the price. It was all huckster garbage.

This is… technically part of S&C Plastics I guess? Montgomery Reynolds and Katherine Sinclair both appear, so…

Thanks to everyone who critted this, including Taffeta, gishface, DrLeibowitz and Westrin.

Image Attribution: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Necronomicon_prop.jpg Created by Wikimedia user "Shubi". Image released into the public domain by its creator.
